---
title: TTL
description: Time-to-live format — string shortcuts and millisecond numbers.
icon: Timer
---

## Format

TTL values can be expressed as a **string** (human-readable shorthand), a **number** (milliseconds from now), or a **`Date`** (absolute expiry time).

### String format

```ts
await cache.set("key", value, { ttl: "5m" });
```

| Unit | Suffix | Example |
|------|--------|---------|
| Seconds | `s` | `"30s"` |
| Minutes | `m` | `"5m"` |
| Hours | `h` | `"1h"` |
| Days | `d` | `"7d"` |

Each string must contain exactly one value and one unit (e.g. `"5m"`, `"1h"`, `"7d"`). Multi-unit strings like `"1h30m"` are **not supported** and will be treated as no TTL.

### Number format

Pass the duration in **milliseconds** from now:

```ts
await cache.set("key", value, { ttl: 60_000 }); // 1 minute
```

### Date format

Pass a `Date` to set an **absolute expiry time**:

```ts
const expiresAt = new Date("2025-12-31T23:59:59Z");
await cache.set("key", value, { ttl: expiresAt });
```

## parseTtl

Convert a TTL value to milliseconds (or a `Date` for absolute expiry) using the exported `parseTtl` utility:

```ts
import { parseTtl } from "@justwant/cache";

parseTtl("5m");      // → 300000  (number, ms)
parseTtl("1h");      // → 3600000 (number, ms)
parseTtl(60_000);    // → 60000   (pass-through)
parseTtl(new Date()) // → Date    (pass-through)
parseTtl(undefined)  // → undefined
```

The return type is `number | Date | undefined`. Invalid strings return `undefined`.

## Default TTL

Set a default TTL at cache creation time. Individual `set` / `wrap` calls override it:

```ts
const cache = createCache({
  adapter: memoryAdapter(),
  defaults: { ttl: "1h" },
});

// Uses default TTL
await cache.set("a", 1);

// Overrides with 5 minutes
await cache.set("b", 2, { ttl: "5m" });
```

## No expiration

Omit `ttl` and do not set a default to store a value permanently (until explicitly deleted):

```ts
await cache.set("config:feature-x", enabled);
```
